name: Credential Enrollment Intent
summary: Thin, UX-only seam for the credential-enrollment moment — the create() / registration counterpart to the web-identity (sign-in) intent. Owns only the free authorial UX choices the technical ceremony does NOT determine: when to prompt enrollment (moment), whether the user may defer (skippability), the motivational tone of the ask, and the request surface. The technical credential-creation axes (userVerification, residentKey, authenticatorAttachment, attestation, timeout) ride the CustomCredentialProvider config + #483 config-flavors and are referenced here as constraints, never re-homed. Composes the Loader intent (in-flight create()), the Feedback intent (enroll success/failure), and the web-identity intent (post-enroll authState transitions to signed-in). No provider, ceremony, or protocol surface — those stay in the deferred webidentity project (#483).

moment.description: When in the journey the enrollment ask is surfaced. Pure placement — tech-independent product/UX policy, not a credential property. The native create() call is identical wherever it fires.

skippability.description: Whether the user may defer or dismiss the enrollment ask. A product policy on the moment, not an authenticator capability — orthogonal to userVerification (which the platform enforces during the ceremony).

tone.description: The motivational framing of the enrollment ask. Enrollment-scoped on purpose: benefit-led sells the upside ('faster sign-in next time'), security-led frames protection ('add a passkey to secure your account'), neutral states the action plainly. A SHARED cross-intent tone axis is an open decision (#1427); precedent (#1318/#1324) rejected flattening tone across intents, so this dimension carries its own enrollment vocabulary and does not claim a shared contract.

surface.description: How the enrollment ask is presented. UX-only and parallel to the web-identity intent's requestSurface; the underlying navigator.credentials.create() call is identical across surfaces.

description:
What This Is

The create() / enrollment counterpart to the Web Identity (sign-in) intent. Where web-identity covers asking for a credential (navigator.credentials.get()), this intent covers the enrollment moment — navigator.credentials.create(), e.g. setting up a passkey. It is thin and UX-only: it standardizes the free authorial choices the technical ceremony does not decide for you.

The collaboration: tech-driven vs free choice

Enrollment is where the technical config and the UX intent interlock. The technical axes determine what ceremony runs and what prompts the platform itself shows — and they are NOT this intent's to own:

  • userVerification, residentKey/discoverable, authenticatorAttachment, attestation, timeout — the policy slots of PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions. These ride the CustomCredentialProvider registry + config-flavors ruled by #483 (credential-management protocol). This intent references them as constraints (e.g. attachment: platform implies a "use this device" framing) but never re-authors them — re-homing them in a product surface would be the duplication #499 rejected.

What is left to the author — and is genuinely under-specified by the platform — is the free UX layer: the four dimensions above (moment, skippability, tone, surface). Every app that enrolls a passkey faces these choices regardless of which authenticator config it ships.

Native anchor

Anchored on the Credential Management create() model and WebAuthn registration (PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions). The intent stops at the enrollment request + its presentation; it never models the provider, the ceremony (challenge / attestation verification / ArrayBuffer juggling), or any wire protocol.

Thin by design — what it is NOT

No provider registry, no FedCM/WebAuthn ceremony surface, no protocol. The full webidentity project + credential-acquisition protocol stay deferred (#483). Consumers that need the ceremony compose the native API (or a SimpleWebAuthn-style adapter) directly; this intent only standardizes the declarative enrollment-request + presentation shape.

Composition (cross-reference, do not duplicate)

  • Loader: the in-flight create() ceremony is an async operation — drive the Loader intent for the spinner/disabled state, do not re-invent progress here.
  • Feedback: enrollment success / failure is announced through the Feedback intent, not a bespoke toast.
  • Web Identity: a successful enrollment transitions the authState to signed-in — read that signal from the web-identity seam, do not invent a parallel session state.

Interface Protocol

type EnrollMoment = 'inline-signup' | 'post-signin-upsell' | 'post-action' | 'dedicated-settings';
type Skippability = 'required' | 'skippable' | 'dismissible';
type EnrollTone = 'benefit-led' | 'security-led' | 'neutral';
type EnrollSurface = 'inline' | 'dialog' | 'redirect';

interface CredentialEnrollmentIntent {
    // When the enrollment ask is surfaced in the journey
    moment: EnrollMoment;
    // Whether the user may defer / dismiss the ask
    skippability: Skippability;
    // Motivational framing of the ask (enrollment-scoped vocabulary)
    tone: EnrollTone;
    // How the ask is presented
    surface: EnrollSurface;
    // Technical creation axes are NOT modelled here: they ride the
    // CustomCredentialProvider config + #483 config-flavors and are read
    // as constraints (userVerification, residentKey, authenticatorAttachment,
    // attestation, timeout).
}
